125609.explanation: We ensure that community centers and sports halls are managed independently so that everyone is welcome. At the same time, we gladly give residents the space to take action themselves on the greenery in their street.

125611.explanation: We want to be a welcoming municipality for all people fleeing war, violence, and oppression.

125616.explanation: To combat the use of illegal fireworks, it would be good if Venlo organized an open-air show. This could also be a laser show or a drone show.

125619.explanation: We believe everyone should be able to participate everywhere. Therefore, we make government buildings as accessible as possible for people with disabilities. For shops or offices, we expect entrepreneurs to arrange this properly themselves.

125620.explanation: For young people this is already happening; for the participation and health of adults, this would also be a good thing.

125621.explanation: For the oversight of democracy, independent journalism is important, also locally.

125623.explanation: He who has the youth has the future. Therefore, the municipality must invest in young talent so that our beautiful local traditions and events are still celebrated many years from now.

125627.explanation: Everyone must feel safe on the street, including women. In places where needed, cameras can help prevent problems. But we do not hang them everywhere just like that, because everyone also has a right to privacy.

125628.explanation: There are no indications that this offers a solution to any enforcement problem.

125629.explanation: Legally, priority cannot be unlimited. For social cohesion, we want to ensure that at least 50% of new rental homes are allocated to people with ties to Venlo and the local (community) life. This way we keep our neighborhoods strong and connected.

125631.explanation: In some neighborhoods of Venlo, it is already mandatory to live in your new home yourself. We want the municipality to make this a strict requirement for all new construction projects from now on. This gives people seeking a home a fair chance.

125634.explanation: Not everyone receiving benefits can just start working. Even though there are many jobs, there are people for whom working really is not possible. We must have understanding for this and not unnecessarily pressure them.

125635.explanation: Stopping the waiver of municipal taxes can have negative effects on the wellbeing of this group of people who are already struggling. It could actually increase their financial worries, and we do not want that.

125641.explanation: This is important for livability in towns and neighborhoods. This obviously does not apply to the Venlo city center.

125643.explanation: Everyone must be able to choose who helps them in care. We do not think it is necessary to have care providers who work only for one religion. However, it is important that caregivers are sensitive to a person's culture and background, as far as practically possible.

125645.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Venlo does not need to go up, but also not down. We want to keep taxes for residents as low as possible, but enough money must remain to properly perform our duties and services.

125648.explanation: According to GL-PvdA, no more large logistics warehouses need to be built. To facilitate SMEs and the relocation of inner-city businesses, space should remain available, and there is currently still enough.

125650.explanation: Only a limited portion of houses goes to status holders. Because they are not on the waiting list, giving priority helps them integrate faster and relieves the overcrowded asylum centers. Thus, we prevent social problems and ensure better flow.

125651.explanation: Removing waste is expensive and current prices do not cover the costs. A small fee helps raise awareness of one's own waste, so together we work towards a cleaner municipality.

125655.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Venlo to meet demand. We do not want any more because this can cause nuisance in the neighborhood. We want to prevent that.

125656.explanation: We want to increase the share of social rental homes in Venlo to 30%. For this, a share of 35% social rental in new construction projects should be sufficient. In addition, we want to promote the flow of, among others, seniors and people with higher incomes.

125658.explanation: Where this is needed for starters and students, this can be a fine solution. But it must be regulated.

125659.explanation: In Venlo, a few more meeting spaces for residents should be added in some locations.

125662.explanation: GL-PvdA wants to limit street parking in the city center. There will still be space for residents, but day-trippers will park on the outskirts of the center. From there, they travel sustainably to the city center. This creates more space for the people who live in the center.

125665.explanation: There must be a fixed place where homeless labor migrants can sleep. We believe that employment agencies should contribute to this and bear joint responsibility, as they bring these people here to work.

125666.explanation: Venlo must do everything possible to prevent young people from going down the wrong path and getting involved in crime. It is smarter and cheaper to properly help young people now than to have to repair the damage caused by crime later.

125667.explanation: We only want wind turbines in locations where it is already established that they truly generate benefits. Moreover, they may only be built if the profits go directly to the local residents and other inhabitants of Venlo.

125668.explanation: New solar fields may only be established if the neighborhood and the residents of Venlo themselves gain financial benefits from them. At the same time, we want many more solar panels installed on rooftops, provided the electricity grid can handle it.

125609.explanation: There are already examples where citizen initiatives are actively involved in managing public spaces (parks and city gardens). In addition, various Foundations already manage community facilities with sports amenities. This has been functioning well so far.

125611.explanation: Every municipality has the legal obligation under the Distribution Act to provide shelter. EENLokaal does not shy away from statutory duties, even if they are difficult or challenging. In doing so, we always keep the interests of our residents in mind.

125616.explanation: The nationwide fireworks ban is coming. However, we want residents to enjoy professional fireworks. By organizing a show through the municipality, you prevent at least some illegal fireworks. This also prevents nuisance and unnecessary fireworks-related injuries.

125619.explanation: EENLokaal sees this as the responsibility of the entrepreneur. However, the municipality must facilitate the issuance of necessary permits where needed.

125620.explanation: There is already a fund for youth under 18 that covers the membership fees for young people. Additionally, there are increasingly many public sports and leisure facilities available where everyone can participate and meet each other.

125621.explanation: A municipality like Venlo deserves a local broadcaster. The broadcaster already receives a contribution from the municipality to bring local news into the homes of our residents. Additional funding must always be weighed against the social importance.

125623.explanation: EENLokaal supports music lessons for every child. But only where parents cannot afford it should the municipality step in. This currently happens through the Youth Sports and Culture Fund via associations.

125627.explanation: Safety is an important priority for EENLokaal. Camera surveillance gives people a better sense of security and helps with detection. It deters people from bad behavior. Privacy must always be safeguarded.

125628.explanation: Community officers (Boa's) are not police officers. There is a difference in training and role. The use of batons lies with the police.

125629.explanation: If possible and allowed by law, EENLokaal favors allocation to local residents. Think of challenges in informal care, elderly care, and youth who want to stay living in their own village or neighborhood.

125631.explanation: To counter the nuisance caused by slumlords and property subdividers, it is indeed important that the house is occupied by the person who buys it. However, this does not apply to all homes and areas within the municipal boundaries. The current policy aligns with this.

125634.explanation: We encourage everyone to participate in society to the best of their ability, no matter how small. This can be through work, but also through volunteer work. However, the word "must" is too strong in this context.

125635.explanation: This carries too great a risk that more people will enter debt restructuring and thus financially strain the municipal budget even more. Additionally, these people become further socially isolated.

125641.explanation: Local entrepreneurship should be rewarded and we must stimulate the local economy.

125643.explanation: The Netherlands faces a shortage of healthcare personnel. Although we at EENLokaal favor culturally sensitive care, the municipality simply cannot promise or guarantee it. Agreeing with this statement would be a false promise.

125645.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in the municipality of Venlo is already among the lowest in the Netherlands. It has also been stabilized for the coming years (until 2028). We are very proud of this. Further reduction would be irresponsible.

125648.explanation: Aged industrial estates and business premises should be made more available for housing and improving the living environment. Existing companies (and estates) must still be able to expand. There is currently no need for new industrial estates.

125650.explanation: EENLokaal is not in favor of preferential treatment for status holders.

125651.explanation: EENLokaal supports twice-yearly free bulky waste collection in villages, neighborhoods, and centers. Making it permanently free would place too much burden on municipal expenses: it is unaffordable.

125655.explanation: Although EENLokaal realizes that a coffeeshop can cause nuisance, we favor the regulation and control of soft drugs. With a coffeeshop, it becomes visible and controllable, which helps combat street dealing.

125656.explanation: With this, Venlo follows the average benchmark of 30% social housing.

125658.explanation: To counter the nuisance caused by slumlords and property subdividers, it is indeed important that homes are not further subdivided into separate rooms.

125659.explanation: EENLokaal finds this important for the livability, cohesion, and connection in neighborhoods and villages.

125662.explanation: This is wishful thinking. Venlo’s city center will never be completely car-free. However, EENLokaal supports a car-reduced city center.

125665.explanation: The current project is — after one year — a success. As a central city, we deal with many labor migrants, also from surrounding municipalities. We do not want them on the streets and have no way to send them away. Ultimately, nearly 85% move on to other work or return home.

125666.explanation: EENLokaal strives for less youth crime through many preventive programs. The goal is to prevent young people from getting involved in crime.

125667.explanation: EENLokaal believes the municipality of Venlo has fulfilled its task with nine large industrial wind turbines within the municipal boundaries.

125668.explanation: There is still sufficient roof space available, including on industrial estates.

125609.explanation: If this can be done responsibly, it could lead to win-win situations!

125611.explanation: Every municipality must take its responsibility.

125616.explanation: We want to wait for this year's New Year's Eve before making policy. Perhaps the new law proves totally unworkable.

125619.explanation: This could be done in consultation with, for example, municipal loans or something similar.

125620.explanation: We already do! We have a youth sports and culture fund for people with limited financial means.

125623.explanation: We have the youth sports and culture fund for this as well.

125627.explanation: The feeling of safety is insufficient in certain parts of the municipality. If camera surveillance can help improve this, we should do it!

125628.explanation: No, first we will wait to see the effects of the bodycams before considering this.

125629.explanation: We believe it is important that this remains.

125631.explanation: Depends on the sub-area! Private rental is also a very valuable step in a housing career.

125634.explanation: Everyone should contribute their part to society.

125635.explanation: People who need help with finances should receive assistance. Forgiving debt is a free pass to not manage money well; as far as we're concerned, this goes one step too far.

125641.explanation: Our residents must be able to use good parking facilities for daily shopping.

125643.explanation: We see this as a basic right.

125645.explanation: This is already extremely low in our municipality.

125648.explanation: No, we need to look at the future and the transition of the current business parks.

125650.explanation: There are national rules for this; it is not for the municipality to decide.

125651.explanation: We believe this will reduce illegal dumping.

125656.explanation: In Venlo, the balance must be restored without endangering the statutory 30 percent. This means scaling down social housing where there is too much and increasing it where there is too little.

125658.explanation: Individual room rentals can be excellent for some buildings; otherwise, you end up with unauthorized subletting.

125662.explanation: The car is and remains crucial for visitors to the city center and for the city center retailers.

125665.explanation: Unfortunately, temporary shelter for homeless labor migrants is necessary because otherwise, we experience nuisance.

125666.explanation: The more youth crime is solved early on, the better!

125667.explanation: Only when there is local support and added value for the local community and it does not come at the expense of valuable agriculture and nature.

125609.explanation: VVD Venlo believes the municipality is there for the residents, not the other way around. We encourage citizen participation where residents themselves come up with plans for social issues.

125611.explanation: We prioritize the safety of residents and entrepreneurs in the reception of asylum seekers. We do not tolerate nuisance, but we do carry out statutory tasks imposed by the national government, provided these come with strict conditions for safety and limited group size.

125616.explanation: VVD Venlo supports a central, professional fireworks show as an alternative to private consumer fireworks. Now that a ban is in effect, the government should provide an alternative in consultation with the neighborhoods and districts.

125619.explanation: VVD Venlo expects entrepreneurs to take responsibility for making their buildings accessible, without financial support from the municipality. The municipality should set an example with municipal buildings.

125620.explanation: VVD Venlo sees a piling up of various allowances, benefits, compensations, and debt forgiveness. Work must remain rewarding, so we will eliminate the tangle of local schemes and move to one additional poverty scheme. The municipality is not an income desk but a springboard.

125621.explanation: VVD Venlo follows the line 'commercial where necessary, cost-saving where possible.' This encourages finding own income sources instead of dependency on municipal support.

125623.explanation: VVD Venlo supports access to cultural education, but through existing educational funding and targeted poverty schemes for those who need it, not through a general municipal subsidy for all children.

125627.explanation: VVD Venlo favors expanding camera surveillance as a means to increase safety and tackle nuisance and crime. The party wants cameras not only in the center but also in other possible risk areas, such as at an asylum seekers center or the stations.

125628.explanation: We ensure our enforcement officers (BOAs) are well equipped and therefore advocate expanding their equipment with defensive tools like pepper spray and baton, so our people can defend themselves appropriately. We emphasize that the safety of the officers themselves is paramount.

125629.explanation: VVD wants to end priority policies and pursue a fair allocation system. Additionally, we want to stop social housing in the inner ring of Venlo because land prices there are high, resulting in relatively expensive homes for the social rental sector.

125631.explanation: Instead of restricting existing rights, VVD Venlo focuses on addressing the housing shortage by building more various housing types (owner-occupied, mid-rent, etc.) and speeding up permit processes. We can make exceptions for a few neighborhoods.

125634.explanation: Participation is the norm. VVD Venlo views social assistance as temporary and as a 'springboard' towards paid employment. A counter-performance can help provide structure and work experience.

125635.explanation: Social provisions and community funds should primarily go to those who need them most. Debt forgiveness is a tool to relieve residents with an income at social assistance level who do not have assets.

125641.explanation: VVD Venlo wants to keep parking affordable and not drive visitors away. At shopping centers, the party opts for good accessibility, sufficient parking space, and no increase in parking costs for residents. Free parking fits that principle.

125643.explanation: VVD Venlo strongly emphasizes customized care and room for both large and small care providers. Freedom of choice belongs to that. As long as providers are reliable and do not commit fraud, it fits the VVD line that residents can choose care that suits their background.

125645.explanation: Local taxes must go down or at least not increase. The property tax (OZB) is explicitly mentioned as a tax that must not be raised. A reduction fits within the VVD policy to relieve residents and limit burdens.

125648.explanation: VVD Venlo opts for growth in manufacturing, technology, and circular companies. This requires extra space and clear zones where entrepreneurs can expand. New or expanded business parks strengthen the economy and create jobs in the region.

125650.explanation: VVD believes social rental homes must be allocated fairly and without priority. In Venlo, VVD wants to end priority policies and opts for an equal allocation policy, so residents do not feel they are at the back of the line.

125651.explanation: VVD wants to keep local taxes low but also chooses a critical waste policy and cost control. Free dumping often leads to higher waste costs for all residents. VVD Venlo wants effective measures that remain affordable without increasing costs.

125655.explanation: We choose strict enforcement and limiting nuisance. In Venlo, the VVD’s starting point is no expansion because the balance between supply, supervision, and neighborhood safety is already under pressure.

125656.explanation: VVD opts for mixing, flow, and freedom of choice, not for high quotas. A mandatory 40% social rental share slows building projects, makes homes more expensive, and increases pressure on scarce land. Our line is: more building in all segments, with emphasis on mid-rent and affordable owner-occupied housing.

125658.explanation: The VVD opts for space in the housing market, not for additional prohibitions. Renting out rooms can actually help make better use of scarce housing, for example for students, starters, or labor migrants. What is important is strict enforcement on nuisance, safety, and quality.

125659.explanation: VVD Venlo wants to support where there is need, and give room to residents who want to set something up. Venlo has sufficient community centers.

125662.explanation: VVD wants an accessible and lively center. A completely car-free center makes Venlo less accessible for visitors, entrepreneurs, and people dependent on cars. That harms both the economy and the attractiveness of the city center.

125665.explanation: VVD Venlo chooses order, safety, and decent housing. Fixed shelter prevents people from ending up on the street, reduces nuisance, and makes it possible to better enforce against unscrupulous landlords. The principle remains: temporary, sober, and aimed at transitioning towards work.

125666.explanation: VVD Venlo wants to work with youth enforcement officers and a top-X approach for criminal youth. All this requires more capacity, not less.

125667.explanation: VVD chooses care, support, and smart energy solutions. New wind turbines often have a large spatial impact in Venlo, cause nuisance, and yield relatively little compared to the drawbacks for local residents. Moreover, Venlo has already delivered its contribution to wind turbines.

125668.explanation: VVD opts for economical use of space and smart energy solutions. Large-scale solar fields occupy much open landscape, affect nature and views, and yield relatively little compared to solar on roofs.

125609.explanation: Privatization of sports halls, parks, or complexes should be investigated.

125611.explanation: At present, we comply with the nationally imposed allocation decision. So no closing, but also no expansion!

125616.explanation: For the safety of its residents.

125619.explanation: If entrepreneurs want to admit disabled people, they must arrange the entrance accordingly.

125620.explanation: Sports are healthy and often such activities are the first to be cut when there is a lack of money.

125621.explanation: In the first place, the broadcaster itself must stand on its own feet.

125623.explanation: Music lessons should be part of every child's regular curriculum, just like sports.

125627.explanation: Camera surveillance has too little effect on the feeling of safety.

125631.explanation: Yes, that is called a self-occupancy obligation. There are too many slumlords around!

125634.explanation: Often people with benefits live very isolated. With such a regulation, these people could become more involved in society and from there grow towards a 'real' job.

125635.explanation: The VSP believes people with a low income already have enough worries.

125643.explanation: Preferably a choice can be made, but a care provider must be able to care for everyone.

125645.explanation: That is not possible, as it is already zero. It will slowly increase from 2027.

125650.explanation: Refugees, as far as we are concerned, have the right to the same treatment as other citizens.

125651.explanation: That is good for the environment.

125655.explanation: Only assess when an application is submitted.

125656.explanation: In the Venlo region, more people live with low incomes and lower education.

125658.explanation: That is market forces. Otherwise, we will get an even bigger housing problem.

125659.explanation: Neighborhood centers are the meeting places in the neighborhood/district.

125662.explanation: It is a pedestrian and residential area.

125665.explanation: But it must partly be paid by employers/agencies.

125666.explanation: There must be more attention for prevention.

125667.explanation: There are enough wind turbines, as agreed!

125668.explanation: There is no room for that.

125609.explanation: Residents often know best what works in their neighborhood. D66 gives space to initiative and trust. This makes facilities better, stronger, and truly from the community.

125611.explanation: D66 is against. We have deliberately chosen for shelter at Bethanië for over 300 people. Now it is important to carry this out well and humanely.

125616.explanation: D66 is in favor. A fireworks ban should come with a good alternative. With (multiple) shows, we make a safe and festive New Year together.

125619.explanation: D66 does not choose an automatic approach. Accessibility is the responsibility of entrepreneurs and is enforced. The municipality can help where necessary, but help is not standard.

125620.explanation: D66 is in favor, but not automatically. We use existing funds and arrangements smartly so that everyone can participate without unnecessarily creating new structures.

125621.explanation: D66 is in favor if needed. Strong local journalism is indispensable for a healthy democracy and oversight of local government.

125623.explanation: D66 is in favor. Music lessons increase creativity, self-confidence, and opportunities. Every child deserves access to culture, regardless of background or financial situation.

125627.explanation: D66 does not choose an automatic approach here. Camera surveillance should only be temporary, targeted, and well justified. Safety must never come at the expense of privacy without clear necessity.

125628.explanation: D66 is strongly opposed. The monopoly on violence belongs to the police. BOAs (public order officers) are there for supervision and contact, not to cover police tasks or shortages.

125629.explanation: D66 does not want barriers around municipalities. But with long waiting lists in social housing, local ties can be justified. Always carefully and limited, without blocking solidarity or housing turnover.

125631.explanation: D66 opposes a broad owner-occupancy requirement. That hinders mid-price rental and transformation in the city center. Renting is also allowed: investing in housing is no problem.

125634.explanation: D66 is in favor. Meaningful work provides rhythm, self-worth, and opportunities. Always with tailor-made solutions: what someone does must fit their talents, health, and situation.

125635.explanation: D66 opposes this. Forgiveness prevents debts and stress among people with low incomes. Participation starts with basic security, not with extra burdens.

125641.explanation: D66 does not adhere to a fixed dogma. What works differs per area. We consider per location what is needed for accessibility, livability, and strong shopping centers.

125643.explanation: D66 opposes this demand. Due to major staff shortages, availability is currently most important. Good, professional care for everyone comes first.

125645.explanation: D66 opposes this. We choose stable municipal finances so we can invest in housing, amenities, and livability. We are not fans of free promises.

125648.explanation: D66 opposes expansion for the sake of expansion. We prefer intensifying and improving existing sites, with space for entrepreneurs without additional impact on the landscape.

125650.explanation: D66 is in favor. Status holders must leave the asylum seekers' center quickly to build their lives. Setting people against each other does not help. Building more homes does.

125651.explanation: D66 is opposed. Nothing is free. Those who pollute pay. This encourages waste separation, prevents waste, and keeps the system fair for everyone.

125655.explanation: D66 chooses freedom and sensible rules. Not banning, but regulating. Transparency and supervision work better than pushing everything into illegality.

125656.explanation: D66 is strongly opposed here. Strict quotas block housing construction. Venlo actually needs more owner-occupied and mid-price rental housing for turnover. Building must be faster, not stuck in dogmas.

125658.explanation: D66 opposes this ban. Room rental helps reduce the housing shortage. Fewer rules and smarter use of existing homes quickly provide more living space.

125659.explanation: D66 does not believe in blueprints. Where residents want a meeting place, we make that possible. Where it does not fit, we choose other solutions together.

125662.explanation: Fewer cars mean more space for people. D66 chooses a green, livable, and attractive center where you enjoy doing business, living, shopping, and staying.

125665.explanation: D66 chooses shelter instead of fines: no punishment for homelessness, but a roof over your head. This way we provide a humane and safe solution for everyone.

125666.explanation: D66 is in favor. Not with harsher punishments, but with prevention: good education, opportunities, guidance, and perspective. Those with a future do not choose crime.

125667.explanation: D66 chooses clean local energy. With wind turbines, good participation, and local ownership, we make Venlo more sustainable and less dependent on fossil energy.

125668.explanation: D66 chooses clean local energy. With solar panels, good participation, and local ownership, we make Venlo more sustainable and less dependent on fossil energy.

125616.explanation: No firework ban. Better approach to illegal fireworks, better controls at the border.

125620.explanation: Not immediately. But people who have been on social assistance longer and have children should have their children's membership fees reimbursed.

125623.explanation: For low incomes, yes. Residents who can afford it should cover their own costs. For example, up to 130% of the minimum income can qualify for support.

125627.explanation: In some places with much nuisance, yes. For example, camera surveillance may be expanded in the future at the asylum seekers' center.

125628.explanation: Baton, and if possible also handcuffs and pepper spray.

125629.explanation: Correct, no priority for status holders.

125631.explanation: Owner-occupancy requirement in the entire municipality. No more room for investment companies and house flippers. Also, strict controls on overcrowding.

125634.explanation: For people with the ability, a performance requirement can certainly be set.

125635.explanation: Up to 130% there should be the possibility. For people returning to work, there should be a phase-out so that the gap between benefits and work is rewarding.

125641.explanation: Basic necessities should not be made unnecessarily more expensive.

125643.explanation: Essential aid must remain available to everyone. No religion, culture, or belief may be a condition.

125648.explanation: More work for people in benefit positions. No more block housing.

125651.explanation: At least significantly cheaper. The municipality itself wants people to sort their waste.

125655.explanation: No expansion, maintain previous deterrence policy.

125656.explanation: Perhaps even higher, given that the demand is high.

125659.explanation: It was agreed upon in the council but the Executive Board did not adhere to this agreement.

125662.explanation: As a result, market traders are no longer motivated to come to Venlo. A local entrepreneur must be free to bring goods to their store.

125665.explanation: Return to home country if there is no commitment to work. Shelter can be temporary if there is a corresponding performance.

125666.explanation: Ensure that crime is not profitable.

125667.explanation: No additional wind turbines. Costs are only money that the residents of Venlo have to pay.

125668.explanation: No solar fields on the land.

125635.explanation: We want to significantly reduce municipal charges first, before stopping the waiving of taxes.

125609.explanation: If such an initiative arises, the municipality can assess whether it is realistic. Subsequently, it should be possible.

125611.explanation: The current asylum shelter in our municipality, namely the shelter on Emmastraat, has proven to be without any nuisance. As far as we are concerned, this location may remain.

125616.explanation: As a result of the fireworks ban, it would be good if our municipality offers an alternative.

125619.explanation: A small subsidy measure that gives entrepreneurs an incentive to make this possible themselves is, in our opinion, a good solution.

125620.explanation: Agree, in many cases there is also some form of contribution. Fully reimbursing membership by making agreements with certain gyms is, according to DENK, a good measure.

125621.explanation: Local broadcasters must be able to manage with the existing funding streams.

125623.explanation: Primary school children whose parents cannot afford it can be supported in this.

125627.explanation: There should be more camera surveillance in dangerous places, but in other places the privacy of our residents is central.

125628.explanation: The necessity of a baton for enforcement officers must be addressed together with additional training on its use. We do not see this as feasible in the short term in our municipality.

125629.explanation: DENK believes that 50% of social rental homes must be reserved in advance for housing seekers from our own municipality.

125631.explanation: Owner-occupancy obligation must become the standard throughout the municipality.

125634.explanation: First of all, it must be checked whether this fits within the individual living situation of the resident. If this appears possible, it can be an option. However, DENK opposes making it mandatory.

125635.explanation: Residents with a low income already find it difficult to make ends meet. Waiving municipal taxes is an essential arrangement for them without which they could not manage at all.

125641.explanation: Parking at shopping centers in villages and neighborhoods must always be and remain free. Parking around Venlo's city center should be made cheaper.

125643.explanation: Culturally sensitive care must be central in providing care. If a resident wants a specific caregiver in this regard, this must be facilitated.

125645.explanation: The property tax rate in our municipality is too high and must be lowered for the average citizen.

125648.explanation: There are sufficient industrial estates in Venlo where valuable companies can settle.

125650.explanation: Status holders must be properly housed. At this moment there is no serious bottleneck in Venlo that makes a priority status valuable, but the option must remain open.

125651.explanation: Disposing of waste that can be well recycled or sold must be free. For certain types of residual waste, a cost-covering tariff can be charged.

125655.explanation: Venlo already has many coffee shops. New coffee shops will not be allowed.

125656.explanation: At least 40% of the homes must be social housing.

125658.explanation: A healthy balance is necessary; for certain groups of residents, renting separate rooms can be an easy form of independent living. We focus mainly on whole houses but do not want to prohibit renting out separate rooms.

125659.explanation: This is a good measure to strengthen livability and social cohesion.

125662.explanation: The city center can become car-free, but not the access roads and surrounding roads.

125665.explanation: The municipality must prioritize humane reception of people in need, but it should not become permanent for employment agencies to evict people onto the street. Employment agencies will be fined where possible.

125666.explanation: By investing in more prevention, we want to reduce youth crime.

125667.explanation: Venlo currently has 9 wind turbines and many surrounding municipalities have none. They must first take their responsibility as well.

125668.explanation: We focus on solar panels on roofs; vacant fields can be better used for, for example, housing development.

125609.explanation: Management of public facilities is a core task of the municipality. This responsibility must not be shifted to volunteers. Residents can support and contribute for compensation, but the municipality remains responsible for quality, safety, and continuity.

125611.explanation: Humane shelter within legal frameworks. - Limits on capacity per neighborhood. - Limit or phase out mega asylum centers - New procedures transparent and with support - No shelter that structurally overburdens Venlo - Limit / revise / bring within limits

125616.explanation: This directly affects affordability and municipal finances. We believe this requires careful consideration. The opinion of residents is currently not sufficiently clear. Therefore, we will first gauge what the people of Venlo want before taking a definitive position.

125619.explanation: Accessibility is important. Entrepreneurs also have their own responsibility in this. The municipality can assist with advice and appropriate financial customization, but structural financial aid does not fit a financially responsible policy.

125620.explanation: This must be tailored. Sports and participation in clubs are important for health and social cohesion. Support should be available for those who really need it, but not through an automatic and unlimited obligation for the municipality.

125621.explanation: Independence must be guaranteed. A local broadcaster plays an important role in providing information. Extra funding can only be provided if there is demonstrable need and within a financially responsible framework. No structural increase without clear justification.

125623.explanation: Music education is valuable, but there should be no automatic subsidy for everyone. Support must focus on equal opportunities. Cooperation with schools and clubs is more appropriate than a generic arrangement.

125627.explanation: If safety demands it, targeted camera surveillance should be possible at known nuisance locations. It is a means to better protect residents, not a goal in itself. Our citizens ask for people, not digital systems.

125628.explanation: The safety of public enforcement officers (BOA’s) is important. Equipment must match the risks encountered in practice. No standard armament, but a substantiated choice based on threat, training, and clear protocols. Remaining visible and approachable is paramount.

125629.explanation: Social rental homes are primarily for residents with ties to Venlo. Priority for local home seekers is logical. This way we protect housing flow and livability.

125631.explanation: A residence obligation helps starters and protects livability. This must be applied within legal frameworks and be targeted for new builds and affordable housing.

125634.explanation: Participation is important. Those who can work should also give something back to society. But customization is essential. Take health, caregiving duties, and distance to the labor market into account.

125635.explanation: Waiving arrears prevents people with low incomes from getting into deeper trouble. Financially responsible policy also means considering affordability. Support must be targeted and fair so that no one needlessly falls into debt.

125641.explanation: Free parking at shopping centers keeps the center attractive and accessible. High parking fees currently form a barrier to a pleasant shopping experience. We must support visitors and entrepreneurs, not drive them away. Vibrant centers require easy accessibility.

125643.explanation: Appropriate care is paramount. Freedom of choice is important but should not lead to unworkable or unaffordable policies. The municipality must operate within legal and financial frameworks.

125645.explanation: Municipal taxes on homes should remain as low as possible. Residents feel the pressure on their wallets. Reduction can only occur if the budget is in order and services are not undermined. First cut unnecessary expenses, then raise taxes if needed.

125648.explanation: Employment is important, but expansion must be based on demonstrable need. First better utilize existing sites and address vacancies. New business parks only if they spatially fit within amenities and do not harm livability or the landscape.

125650.explanation: Social rental housing is scarce. Giving priority increases pressure on waiting lists and feelings of inequality. Allocation must be fair and explainable, considering affordability and livability. Everyone waiting deserves equal treatment within clear frameworks.

125651.explanation: Waste collection must be affordable and fair. Free drop-off sounds attractive, but costs do not disappear. Prevent dumping and encourage sorting for Venlo residents with targeted exemptions where necessary. Keeping clean is a joint effort.

125655.explanation: No expansion or automatic ban, but livability and support are decisive. A new coffee shop only if location, spread, safety, and neighborhood impact are carefully weighed.

125656.explanation: Affordable housing is important, but one fixed percentage for all projects does not suit customization. The housing mix must align with the needs per neighborhood and village. Managing the balance between social rental, mid-rent, and ownership is more effective than copying a national norm.

125658.explanation: No general ban. Room rentals can contribute to housing for starters but must not strain livability. Tailored solutions per neighborhood with clear quality and rent requirements and attention to parking pressure and nuisance. Living must fit the neighborhood.

125659.explanation: VBI values meeting and social cohesion in neighborhoods. Meeting is important, but policies must suit the neighborhood. Initiatives should come from the neighborhood. By the citizens, for the citizens, through the citizens.

125662.explanation: A fully car-free center harms accessibility for residents, the elderly, and entrepreneurs. Vibrancy requires balance. Reduce nuisance where necessary, but the center must remain accessible to visitors, suppliers, and less mobile residents. Safety before ideology.

125665.explanation: Permanent municipal shelter must not become a structural solution for failing employer responsibilities. Employers are responsible for decent housing and aftercare. Venlo must set limits and prevent neighborhoods from being overloaded. No shelter without clear agreements and support.

125666.explanation: Safety is the foundation of livable neighborhoods. The money Venlo receives for this must be used effectively and lead to concrete results. Focus on prevention, rapid youth aid, and clear enforcement. Help youth in time but also set boundaries.

125667.explanation: No wind turbines over residents’ heads. First support, then decision. - No dogmatic climate approach - No “The Hague dictates Venlo” attitude - But realistically adapting within legal frameworks

125668.explanation: Sustainability is important but not at the expense of livability and support. Solar fields only if they fit spatially, provide local benefits, and residents are involved beforehand. First utilize roofs and business parks, then open landscapes.

125609.explanation: The SP is fed up with cutting public services. Examples of this offer no long-term solution; with declining volunteers and rising or unforeseen costs, the municipality is looked to again. The municipality itself has, among others, staff, knowledge, budget, and maintenance for this.

125611.explanation: There is currently no asylum shelter, only status holders are housed according to legal agreements that Venlo must also comply with. The SP wants fairer distribution of refugees and status holders over all neighborhoods, on a small scale and with dignity.

125616.explanation: Is this a municipal task and can the costs be (structurally) budgeted? Does the population even want such a show, and is it only in Venlo or also in other districts, how to organize? Many unanswered questions, first discuss and listen to what residents want.

125619.explanation: All buildings must be openly accessible. We see this does not frequently arise naturally, so the government must step in where necessary. But not take over that responsibility because primarily it is the company's own task. After all, it is also about their image, service, and customer friendliness.

125620.explanation: Everyone has the right to sport and exercise; the municipality must encourage and promote this. Costs should not be a barrier. After all, we want physically and mentally healthy and vital people. Those costs pay off in the long run (less healthcare costs, etc.).

125621.explanation: The local broadcaster is vital, not only for objective news but also for reporting events (such as sports, culture) and preserving identity (including language). Costs only increase; the municipal contribution should grow accordingly.

125623.explanation: For example, music lessons are not always available or possible (due to scheduling, staff) whereas music enriches children and is useful in other areas. We also want to encourage music clubs to remain vital, including attracting new members (like enthusiastic children).

125627.explanation: Cameras do not catch thieves. It is good to have offenses on video for evidence and legal prosecution, but at the moment itself, cameras must be continuously monitored. (Temporary) cameras can help with nuisance control but are often no permanent solution.

125628.explanation: The safety of BOA's must not be a discussion, and if it helps them perform their duties it can be considered. However, weapons are reserved for the police, and the municipality cannot allocate arms without national legislation.

125629.explanation: Housing is not a profit model but a basic right. The housing shortage in Venlo affects tenants, starters, families, and elderly. We will not build for accommodating expats of large corporations. Venlo home seekers and target groups get priority. This also keeps youth in, for example, their own village.

125631.explanation: Housing is not a profit or investment object. When you buy a house, you do so to live in it. Vacancy is actively addressed. Slumlords and excessive private landlords are strongly combated.

125634.explanation: Nonsense, people are not on welfare for fun. These people are often surviving and experiencing a lot of stress. Stop bullying people by demanding something in return. Many on social minima already often do volunteer work, precisely for self-worth and to help others.

125635.explanation: Especially people with a social minimum can be better supported, also through municipal expenses. Budget-wise this is possible because tax revenues cover costs. This way, these people retain money to provide for their living and pay other high fixed costs.

125641.explanation: Affordable parking for residents and visitors. Parking is a basic service, not a cash cow. Municipal control over parking. Commercial parking companies should not set prices without oversight. Free parking encourages longer stays and more visitors, from which everyone benefits.

125643.explanation: People must have quick and easy access to appropriate care. Care providers should have the space to truly help people, without production and profit pressure. Separate care for target groups tends towards market forces, which must be stopped; good care must be accessible to everyone.

125645.explanation: Municipal taxes exist to fund facilities. In recent years, the property tax (OZB) has remained low, but that seems unsustainable. Taxes must not rise disproportionately and push people into financial distress; housing is not a cash cow. Additionally, tax exemption must remain possible.

125648.explanation: There is little to no land available left for new industrial estates. First, look at existing sites and how to use them better.

125650.explanation: That is legal policy and beyond the municipality's authority. The housing shortage is severe; home seekers and target groups in Venlo are given priority for housing.

125651.explanation: This also encourages increased use. It also prevents illegal dumping of waste and the rise of littering and environmental pollution.

125655.explanation: The tolerance policy is unsustainable: soon regulate and legalize cannabis cultivation. Remove soft drugs from the criminal circuit; through coffee shops you maintain supervision (of quality and health) but ensure support and prevent nuisance. A coffee shop near the border was always a success.

125656.explanation: The SP wants at least 40% social rental housing in every new construction project, without (re)sales.

125658.explanation: We now see excesses in both cases: rooms as well as whole buildings packed with tenants (labor migrants) who pay very high rents. We must combat this. Subletting rooms like a landlady we allow.

125659.explanation: Among others, community centers are invaluable; the municipality must invest in them. They contribute to social cohesion, well-being, equal opportunities, and health. These facilities must never disappear or become unaffordable. They belong to all of us.

125662.explanation: A sympathetic but unfeasible proposal, especially without offering an alternative. It would drive away inner-city residents and entrepreneurs. However, we should examine which areas can be made low-traffic, as is already done with set times for, for example, deliveries.

125665.explanation: Unfortunately, that is urgently needed. Labor migrants are not second-class citizens and should not be used as disposable workers. Provide timely and humane shelter for labor migrants at risk of being homeless. Essentially, this is a task for the companies that bring them here.

125666.explanation: No repression without perspective: we invest in community centers, prevention, youth work, and sports facilities as alternatives to crime.

125667.explanation: Additional wind turbines are not necessary. Onshore wind turbines cause nuisance, provide too little local benefit, and are often imposed by large energy companies. The SP remains critical of possible health risks, shadow flicker, nuisance, and the negative impact on birds and insects.

125668.explanation: The SP opts for sustainable energy from the ground up, in local hands. Sustainability only works if residents have a say and the profits stay in the neighborhood. Solar panels on as many roofs as possible, including rental homes, businesses, and schools, are better than sacrificing (agricultural) land.

125609.explanation: As long as the public interest is served.

125611.explanation: We comply with the number as laid down in the allocation decision, and there are no problems or other special circumstances.

125616.explanation: When neighborhoods or districts desire it, they can apply for an exemption.

125619.explanation: The municipality should facilitate this so that it can be realized in the most accessible manner possible.

125620.explanation: There are various municipal schemes for this target group. A form of choice package could be considered, whereby the municipality supports this group up to a maximum amount, maintaining distinctions and serving the intended purpose.

125621.explanation: Our local media can be more employed to neutrally fulfill the necessary power position within democracy. This requires funding.

125623.explanation: Many music ensembles offer music lessons. When support is needed, we must provide it to the associations.

125628.explanation: Unfortunately, we must better support our enforcement officers (BOAs) here so that they can also more effectively protect themselves against undesirable behavior from residents and visitors.

125629.explanation: For our social welfare, it is important that our own family members can continue living in our own municipality.

125631.explanation: We believe that people should be able to decide for themselves whether they want to live in owner-occupied housing.

125634.explanation: This keeps people socially engaged or prevents them from becoming disengaged and creates more opportunities for potential paid jobs. When people cannot manage on their own, we must of course support them.

125635.explanation: Here we must investigate what possibilities exist to support this target group in managing their finances.

125641.explanation: Rates must fit the environment, so inner-city parking may cost money, while shopping centers outside the centers should be free.

125643.explanation: If there is strong demand, we could explore how to facilitate this without incurring extra costs.

125645.explanation: Venlo has not increased fees for quite some time. In our opinion, there is a considerable backlog in the maintenance of our roads, greenery, and facilities.

125648.explanation: There is still sufficient space in our existing industrial estates.

125650.explanation: We are for equal treatment. So join the end of the waiting lists.

125651.explanation: This leads to less illegal dumping of waste.

125655.explanation: Originally intended to combat street dealing. This does not succeed and also gives the impression that drug use would be normal.

125656.explanation: We more than meet the standard. More regional coordination could be applied here, as surrounding municipalities lag behind Venlo.

125658.explanation: Renting out rooms leads to undesirable occupancy in a neighborhood. These homes are not intended for this, resulting in annoying nuisance for neighbors.

125659.explanation: This is the engine of our society within a neighborhood/district.

125662.explanation: Outside store opening hours (until 1 hour before opening and 1 hour after closing), destination traffic may enter the center.

125665.explanation: We want to prevent homeless labor migrants from the region from coming to Venlo. We want to ensure that employers in this sector take responsibility and ensure that these people are humanely accommodated or returned to their country of origin.

125666.explanation: We must help youth to stay on or get on the right path.

125667.explanation: First fully cover the roofs and then look at other forms of energy generation, such as, for example, an SMR plant.

125668.explanation: First fully cover the roofs and then look at other forms of energy generation, such as, for example, an SMR plant.

statements.title: Venlo must cooperate with the placement of new wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Venlo moet meewerken aan de plaatsing van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Venlo.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
Venlo must put more money into tackling juvenile crime.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Venlo must cooperate with the placement of new solar fields.

titleParts.text: Venlo moet meewerken aan de plaatsing van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Field of solar panels.

statements.title: The BOAs who monitor safety in Venlo must be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Venlo controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Venlo must provide a permanent shelter for homeless migrant workers.

titleParts.text: Venlo moet een vaste opvang voor dakloze

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
Parking at shopping centers must remain free.
At least 40% of newly built homes in Venlo must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Venlo moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality of Venlo must allow more business parks.
The center of Venlo must become car-free.
